data.narrative:
Latitude is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised €50M to date. The peer universe comprises five private companies at Series B/C stage (Sierra Space, Cowboy Space Corporation, Varda Space Industries, Aalyria, and Skyryse) plus six public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL, and one additional ticker).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable triangulation method here. The five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers trade at a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range of $1.1B (Skyryse) to $8.0B (Sierra Space). This median reflects both early-stage growth dynamics and investor appetite in the sector. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 25.8x across six tickers) cannot be applied, as Latitude's revenue is not disclosed—a typical constraint in private company analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. This range reflects the peer-median floor and a modest upside adjustment to account for stage and capital raised. Key uncertainties include Latitude's current revenue run rate, customer concentration, and technical/regulatory milestones achieved since last funding. The wide dispersion in peer valuations ($1.1B–$8.0B) underscores sector-specific risk and the importance of company-level fundamentals in narrowing the range.
